meaning: n.支柱,带子,振作精神vt.支住,撑牢,振作起来,奋起adj.曲柄的v.支持
examples: Overhead, the lights showed the old timbers, used to brace the roof.
Once they made him wear a brace but he fretted so he was downright ill.
We have an internal brace that prevents the tissues from stretching but still allows you to mobilize the joint with some confidence.
If you can't come up with the money in time, brace yourself for a financial nightmare.
Randolph said Griffin was pulling him down so he tried to brace for the fall.
